metadata.scenario: A daughter calls extremely upset because the home health aide didn't show up this morning. Her mother missed medication. She demands a supervisor callback within the hour or she's switching agencies. Very angry tone throughout.

transcription.text: Thank you for calling CareFirst Home Health, this is Margaret. How can I help you today? Hi, Margaret. This is Patricia Wilson. I'm really upset right now! I'm sorry to hear that, Patricia. What seems to be the problem? The home health aide was supposed to be here this morning for my mother, and she never showed up! I understand how frustrating that must be. Let’s get to the bottom of this. My mother missed her medication because of this! I can't believe it! I really apologize for the inconvenience. Can you provide me with your mother's name and the aide’s name? My mother’s name is Eleanor Wilson, and the aide is Lisa. We were counting on her today! Thank you for that information. Let me check our records for you, just a moment. This is unacceptable! If I don’t hear back from a supervisor within the hour, I’m switching agencies! I completely understand your concerns, Patricia. I will escalate this immediately. You better! My mother’s health is at stake here! Absolutely, and I take this very seriously. I'll get you in touch with Angela Martinez, our Client Services Manager. Yes, I need to speak to a supervisor right now! I’ll have Angela call you within the hour. Can I get your number again, please? It’s +13165558471. I hope she calls right away! Thank you, Patricia. I have that noted. I also want to ensure your mother receives her medication. Yes! She needs it! This is so stressful for both of us! I understand, and I’m truly sorry. I can arrange for a nurse to come by today if needed for her medication. That would be helpful, but I need to confirm with Angela first! Of course, I will prioritize this for Angela. She will provide you with all the details. Thank you. I really hope this is resolved quickly. You’re welcome, Patricia. We value your trust, and we want to make this right. I just want my mother to be taken care of. I completely understand. I’ll make sure Angela addresses your concerns promptly. Thank you. I’ll be waiting for her call. Thank you for your patience, Patricia. Have a good day. I hope so. Goodbye. Goodbye.
